summary: Israel conducted multiple strikes against Hezbollah targets in southern Lebanon, including a command centre in Tyre, while Iran reported reopening missile‑tunnel entrances and an explosion on Qeshm Island. Hostile aircraft intrusions were logged along the northern Israeli border and a major Israeli plan for extensive strikes across Lebanon was discussed. Concurrently, West Bank incidents involved a car‑ramming attack at Gush Etzion and medical casualties were reported.
key_points: Israel struck a Hezbollah command centre in the Tyre area and continued air attacks on Hezbollah sites in southern Lebanon (Source: @manniefabian/58336).
Iran announced it has reopened 50 of 69 tunnel entrances at underground missile facilities, including sites near Isfahan and Khomin (Source: @VahidOnline/75817).
Hostile aircraft intrusions were recorded at multiple northern border points, including Rosh HaNikra and Betzet Beach, triggering red alerts (Source: @tzevaadom_en/67882).
An explosion was reported on Qeshm Island in Hormozgan Province (Source: @shin_persian/6323).
Israeli officials are leaning toward approving large‑scale strikes across Lebanon within 24 hours, with civilian evacuation warnings (Source: @Faytuks_Network/3210).
A car‑ramming attack at the Gush Etzion Junction in the West Bank injured several civilians, prompting medical response (Source: @manniefabian/58346).
IRGC targeted “separatist groups” locations in northern Iraq, according to IRIB (Source: @Faytuks_Network/3213).

sections.executive_summary: Israel struck a Hezbollah command centre in Tyre and continued air attacks on southern Lebanon targets. Iran reopened dozens of underground missile‑facility tunnel entrances and reported an explosion on Qeshm Island. Hostile aircraft intrusions and a planned large‑scale Lebanese operation heighten regional tension.

points: State‑owned Mehr News reported an explosion on Qeshm Island in Hormozgan Province, confirming a blast event on the island (Source: @shin_persian/6323).

CNN cited satellite images showing Iran has reopened 50 of 69 tunnel entrances at underground missile facilities, including sites outside Isfahan and around Khomin, after prior US‑Israeli air strikes (Source: @VahidOnline/75817).
Iranian air‑defence systems were activated on Kish Island, as noted by a local post (Source: @lelotsenzura/92393).
Iranian officials warned that reopening tunnel entrances could enable increased ballistic missile launches toward Israel and other countries (Source: @VahidOnline/75817).
IDF released footage of Golani Brigade forces operating in the Bofar fort, indicating active ground operations near the Lebanese border (Source: @idf_telegram/24035).
The Israeli Air Force struck a Hezbollah command centre in the Tyre area and continues strikes on Hezbollah sites in southern Lebanon (Source: @manniefabian/58336).
Israeli forces reported neutralising militants in close‑quarters combat in southern Lebanon, with video evidence shared on social media (Source: @pkpoi/142332).

Israeli officials, including PM Netanyahu and Defense Minister Katz, are considering major strikes across Lebanon within 24 hours, with civilian evacuation plans coordinated with the United States (Source: @Faytuks_Network/3210).
A hostile aircraft intrusion was logged at Rosh HaNikra and other northern border points, prompting red‑alert status (Source: @tzevaadom_en/67882).
The IDF reported a car‑ramming attack at the Gush Etzion Junction in the West Bank, resulting in injuries and a security response (Source: @manniefabian/58346).
Medical teams treated two injured individuals at the Gush Etzion junction incident, with one in serious condition (Source: @yediotnews25/130213).
A separate report described a vehicle‑ramming attack at the Gush Etzion junction, confirming the attacker was neutralised (Source: @yediotnews25/130212).
Emergency services reported four injured persons, two in serious condition, following a recent incident in the Gush Etzion area (Source: @yediotnews25/130215).
Israeli forces engaged Hezbollah militants in southern Lebanon, with footage showing close‑quarters combat and neutralisation of attackers (Source: @pkpoi/142332).
The Israeli military announced strikes on Hezbollah infrastructure in the town of Tzur and other southern Lebanese locations (Source: @almogboker78/17231).
A post celebrated the liberation of the Bofar fort by Golani troops in Lebanon, urging further advances toward Beirut (Source: @rodast_omiddana/72217).
Hezbollah‑linked rocket fire from southern Lebanon was reported to have struck occupied Palestinian territories (Source: @rasedal3ado138e/364305).
Israeli officials are preparing large‑scale operations across Lebanon, potentially affecting hundreds of thousands of civilians (Source: @Faytuks_Network/3210).
IRGC reportedly targeted “separatist groups” in northern Iraq, according to IRIB (Source: @Faytuks_Network/3213).
Iraqi security forces seized 24 illegal Starlink devices at Baghdad International Airport, indicating heightened enforcement (Source: @naya_foriraq/76518).
A major security operation in Karbala, Iraq, resulted in mass arrests of members of the Al‑Sarkhi group (Source: @naya_foriraq/76556).
Preliminary reports indicated a security incident at the Al‑Koot airbase in eastern Iraq (Source: @naya_foriraq/76553).
The French Foreign Minister stated that the Strait of Hormuz lies within international waters and must remain open without fees or extortion (Source: @Faytuks_Network/3206).
Iranian telecom operators Irancell and Hamrah Aval launched installment purchase options for internet packages, reflecting domestic economic activity (Source: @rodast_omiddana/72204).

key_developments: Israel struck a Hezbollah command centre in the Tyre area and continued air attacks on Hezbollah sites in southern Lebanon (Source: @manniefabian/58336).
Iran announced it has reopened 50 of 69 tunnel entrances at underground missile facilities, including sites near Isfahan and Khomin (Source: @VahidOnline/75817).
Hostile aircraft intrusions were recorded at multiple northern border points, including Rosh HaNikra and Betzet Beach, triggering red alerts (Source: @tzevaadom_en/67882).
An explosion was reported on Qeshm Island in Hormozgan Province (Source: @shin_persian/6323).
Israeli officials are leaning toward approving large‑scale strikes across Lebanon within 24 hours, with civilian evacuation warnings (Source: @Faytuks_Network/3210).
A car‑ramming attack at the Gush Etzion Junction in the West Bank injured several civilians, prompting medical response (Source: @manniefabian/58346).

sections.threat_assessment: The operational tempo has intensified across multiple fronts. Israeli air and ground operations in southern Lebanon and along the northern border increase the risk of broader escalation with Hezbollah. Iran's restoration of missile‑facility tunnel access enhances its strategic strike capability, potentially altering deterrence calculations. Concurrent security incidents in Iraq and the West Bank add layers of instability that could be exploited by non‑state actors.
sections.regional_response: Israel is coordinating with the United States on potential large‑scale strikes in Lebanon, while also reinforcing border defenses after multiple hostile aircraft intrusions. Iran is publicly showcasing its missile infrastructure recovery, likely to deter external pressure and reassure domestic audiences. Regional actors such as France are emphasizing freedom of navigation in the Strait of Hormuz, reflecting broader concerns over maritime security.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring of tunnel reopening activities at Iranian missile sites should focus on satellite imagery for construction progress. Israeli operational plans for Lebanon require close watch on civilian evacuation notices and potential humanitarian impacts. The West Bank car‑ramming incident underscores the need for heightened security at high‑traffic junctions. IRGC actions in northern Iraq suggest a willingness to project power beyond Iran's borders, warranting increased surveillance of cross‑border movements.
